batch_summary: Rachel angrily confronts Ross in front of their friends after discovering they are still married because he failed to get an annulment. Ross defends his actions by admitting he couldn't face another failed marriage, while Chandler and Phoebe react with humor and disbelief. When Ross tries to implicate Phoebe, she quickly denies it. Rachel storms out in frustration, and Joey's attempt to defend Ross falls flat. The confrontation is followed by the show's upbeat opening credits sequence.
cumulative_summary: In Monica's apartment, a major confrontation unfolds when Rachel and Ross arrive, and Rachel furiously reveals to the group that Ross never got their annulment, meaning they are still legally married. The news shocks Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe. Ross desperately tries to justify his deception, confessing that he simply could not face the embarrassment of another failed marriage. Chandler cracks jokes about the situation, while Phoebe expresses her outrage. When Ross attempts to deflect blame by claiming Phoebe already knew, Phoebe quickly calls him out on his lie. Rachel, completely fed up with Ross's excuses, storms out of the apartment. Joey tries to offer a defense for Ross but ultimately gives up and heads to the refrigerator. This tense yet comedic confrontation transitions into the show's iconic opening credits sequence, featuring the main cast dancing around a fountain to the theme song.

batch_summary: At Central Perk, Joey finds a set of Porsche keys and decides to find the car to leave a note, discovering his own lost shoe in the lost-and-found box along the way. Meanwhile, Phoebe tricks Monica and Chandler into helping her babysit Frank and Alice's triplets by initially claiming they are puppies. Monica eagerly agrees, viewing it as practice for the future, while a reluctant Chandler is dragged along. Joey's search for the Porsche hits a snag when he locates the wrong car.
cumulative_summary: Following the dramatic confrontation over Ross and Rachel's ongoing marriage, the focus shifts to lighter comedic subplots at Central Perk. Joey discovers a set of Porsche keys left behind and, while searching the lost-and-found, hilariously recovers his own missing shoe. Determined to find the car's owner, Joey heads outside to leave a note but struggles to locate the correct vehicle. Meanwhile, Phoebe approaches Monica and Chandler with a sneaky proposition, tricking them into agreeing to watch "puppies"—which she quickly reveals are actually Frank and Alice's triplets. Though Phoebe is terrified of babysitting alone, Monica enthusiastically agrees, hinting it will be great practice for their own future, while a highly skeptical Chandler is reluctantly dragged into the plan.

batch_summary: Rachel panics about having nowhere to live, prompting Phoebe to offer her a room while her mysterious roommate, Denise, is away. Soon after, Ross arrives with court forms for their annulment. Rachel, furious about his deception, takes charge of the paperwork. When Ross tries to defend his actions by explaining his fear of having three failed marriages, Rachel angrily points out that she is now halfway to that milestone herself, leaving their relationship highly strained.
cumulative_summary: Following the dramatic confrontation over Ross and Rachel's ongoing marriage, Joey finds Porsche keys at Central Perk and tries to locate the car, while Phoebe tricks Monica and Chandler into babysitting her brother's triplets. Meanwhile, Rachel panics about having nowhere to live, leading Phoebe to offer her a room while her roommate, Denise, is out of town. The peace is short-lived when Ross arrives with annulment forms. Still furious over his deception, Rachel snatches the paperwork to handle it her way. When Ross attempts to justify his actions by explaining his fear of having three failed marriages, Rachel bitterly points out that, thanks to him, she is now halfway to that milestone herself. Their confrontation ends in high tension as Ross jokingly compares her anger to their infamous "on a break" dispute.

batch_summary: Joey locates the silver Porsche matching the keys he found. After successfully unlocking it, he decides to pretend the luxury car is his to impress a passing woman, leaning into the lie with flirtatious charm. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, and Chandler struggle to manage the triplets. Feeling overwhelmed, Chandler proposes a 'zone defense' strategy where Phoebe and Monica handle the messy chores of wiping and diapering, while he takes the easy job of admiring how cute the babies are.
cumulative_summary: Following the dramatic confrontation over Ross and Rachel's ongoing marriage, Joey tracks down the silver Porsche matching the keys he found. Unable to resist the allure of the luxury vehicle, he pretends it is his to impress a passing woman, leaning into a flirtatious lie. Meanwhile, the babysitting situation with Phoebe's brother's triplets gets underway. As Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler struggle to manage the infants, Phoebe admits to feeling overwhelmed. Chandler humorously proposes a 'zone defense' strategy to make things run smoother. However, his plan is highly self-serving: he suggests that Phoebe handle the wiping and Monica take care of the diapering, leaving him with the sole, easy responsibility of admiring how cute the babies are.

batch_summary: While Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler struggle on the floor to diaper the triplets, Joey returns to the apartment thrilled by the attention his fake Porsche ownership brings. He borrows Monica's cleaning supplies—which she eagerly provides due to her obsessive cleaning habits—to wash the car. Back outside, Joey continues to boast about "his" car to passersby until the actual owner suddenly returns and catches him in the act. Desperate to keep up the charade, Joey hilariously begs for five more minutes.
cumulative_summary: Following the dramatic confrontation over Ross and Rachel's ongoing marriage, Joey tracks down a silver Porsche matching keys he found and pretends it is his. Meanwhile, Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler struggle to babysit the triplets. Despite Chandler's attempt to avoid dirty work with a self-serving "zone defense" plan, Phoebe forces him to help change the babies. Joey returns to the apartment thrilled by the attention his fake car ownership brings. He borrows Monica's cleaning supplies—which she eagerly provides due to her obsessive cleaning habits—to wash the vehicle. Back outside, Joey continues to boast about "his" car to a passerby until the actual owner suddenly returns and catches him in the act. Caught red-handed, Joey hilariously begs the owner for just five more minutes with the car.

batch_summary: Joey's Porsche fantasy ends when the actual owner takes his keys back and drives away. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Monica find babysitting the triplets surprisingly easy. Chandler tries to introduce an action figure as a toy, but Monica and Phoebe warn him it is a choking hazard. Dismissing their concerns, Chandler tries to prove them wrong but ends up swallowing a small part of the toy himself. He returns to the room gagging and choking, forcing him to admit they were right.
cumulative_summary: Following the dramatic confrontation over Ross and Rachel's ongoing marriage, Joey tracks down a silver Porsche matching keys he found and pretends it is his. Meanwhile, Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler struggle to babysit the triplets. Joey's charade with the Porsche officially ends when the owner reclaims his keys and drives off, leaving Joey behind. Back at the apartment, Phoebe and Monica watch over the sleeping triplets, finding the task surprisingly easy. Chandler arrives with an action figure named Krog to show the babies, but Monica and Phoebe immediately deem it unsafe due to small, swallowable parts. Chandler scoffs at their overprotectiveness, recalling his own rough childhood. However, he quickly returns in a panic, admitting they were right after accidentally swallowing the toy's plastic sonic blaster gun while trying to prove his point. The segment ends with Chandler gagging and choking in front of an unimpressed Monica and Phoebe.
